content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 7, 1990


Christian G. Howland
Borton, Petrini & Conron
Westchester Corporate Plaza
1800 - 30th Street, 4th Floor
P.O. Box 2026
Bakersfield, CA 93303

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-443

Dear Mr. Howland:

This is in response to your request for written confirmation
of prior telephone advice regarding the relationship between a
proposed contribution of office space and the contribution provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your question is general in nature, we are treating it as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed).²

QUESTION

Does a contribution of office space from a private nonprofit
organization which is part of a mutual benefit corporation to that
mutual benefit corporation violate any of the provisions of the
Act?

CONCLUSION

A contribution of office space by a nonprofit organization to
a mutual benefit corporation does not violate any provision of the
Act so long as there is no connection between the donation and any
political candidate or campaign.



¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
Regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-443
Page 2

FACTS

You represent a nonprofit, mutual benefit corporation (the
"corporation"), which includes public as well as private entities
in its membership. A private member wishes to donate office space
to the corporation; the corporation's costs for this space would
be limited to its operating expenses. A concern has been raised
that the proposed donation might constitute an illegal political
contribution.

A telephone call to the Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division informed you that the contemplated arrangement regarding
office space would not violate any provision of the Act. The
basis for that advice is your representation that the anticipated
donation of office space has no relationship whatsoever to any
political candidate or campaign. Your letter to us requests written confirmation of the advice previously given to you by
telephone.

ANALYSIS

Section 82015 defines "contribution" as follows (in pertinent
part):

"Contribution" means a payment, a forgiveness
of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or
an enforceable promise to make a payment except to
the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes. (Emphasis added.)

Regulation 18215, copy enclosed, reiterates that a contribution is any monetary or nonmonetary payment made for political
purposes. Where there is no connection of any kind between a
donation and a candidate,³ a ballot measure committee, a
controlled committee, or any organization formed or existing for
political purposes, then there would be no contribution within the
meaning of the Act.

Your letter makes no mention of any connection that would
lead to a conclusion that the proposed donation of office space is
made for political purposes. You assert that there is "no
political candidate or campaign involved in the situation." If
additionally there is no connection between the donor of the office space and any ballot measure committee, any controlled committee, or any organization formed or existing for political

________________________
³ As defined in the Act, the term "candidate" is very broad and
includes elected officers who have not terminated their controlled
committees. (Section 82007.)

File No. I-90-443
Page 3

purposes, then there is no contribution subject to the restric-
tions of the Act.

I trust the above answers your question. If you need further
information, please do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Susan L. Bobrow
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:SLB:plh

Enclosures
